title: Pipelines Output Schema v2.0
description: JSON Schema for the 8 CSV output files produced by the pipelines package. Each pipeline's clean() method returns a pandas DataFrame; the DataFrame is persisted to CSV in the PROCESSED_DATA_DIR. This schema documents the column contracts so downstream consumers (Phase 2 Neo4j graph builder, Phase 3 Graph Transformer) can validate inputs. Audit findings INT-8, INT-9, LIN-4. P1-21 ROOT FIX: chembl_activities_clean.csv (the 8th CSV, emitted by ChEMBLPipeline.clean_activities()) is now declared here — previously it was emitted but undocumented, so downstream consumers could not validate its column contract.

drugs.csv.description: ChEMBL output: small-molecule bioactive compounds. Produced by ChEMBLPipeline.clean() (chembl_pipeline.py).

chembl_activities_clean.csv.description: ChEMBL output: cleaned bioactivity measurements (drug-target pharmacology). Produced by ChEMBLPipeline.clean_activities() (chembl_pipeline.py). Each row is ONE measurement on ONE subunit of a (possibly multi-subunit) target — multi-subunit complexes are exploded so each subunit's UniProt accession is a separate row. activity_value is normalized to nM (S13). The downstream load() step aggregates by (drug, protein, activity_type) using the median activity_value to produce one DPI per pair. P1-21 ROOT FIX: this entry was missing from the original schema, so the file was emitted but its column contract was undocumented.

activity_id.description: ChEMBL activity_id (integer as string). Primary key per measurement.

molecule_chembl_id.description: ChEMBL molecule ID. Foreign key to drugs.csv.chembl_id (filtered to the in-memory drug set during clean_activities()).

target_chembl_id.description: ChEMBL target ID. May be a single protein or a multi-subunit complex.

target_accession.description: UniProt accession resolved from target_chembl_id via ChEMBL /target.json. For multi-subunit complexes, the row is exploded so each accession is a separate row.

target_pref_name.description: ChEMBL preferred target name (for observability; not used downstream).

activity_type.description: Standardised activity type (case-sensitive). Filtered to CHEMBL_ACTIVITY_TYPES during clean_activities().

activity_value.description: Activity value normalised to nM (S13). NULL when normalisation failed (e.g. unsupported source unit).

activity_units.description: Always 'nM' after normalisation. NULL when normalisation failed.

pchembl_value.description: -log10(activity_value in M). Preserved from ChEMBL API (S14).

assay_id.description: ChEMBL assay_chembl_id (foreign key to the assay).

standard_relation.description: Relation between activity_value and the true value. audit-2025 ROOT FIX (issue 5): the previous description claimed all 6 relations pass through, but the pipeline's CHEMBL_STANDARD_RELATIONS filter defaults to {'='} only. The enum is kept for forward-compat (operators can widen the filter via DRUGOS_CHEMBL_STANDARD_RELATIONS env var), but the DEFAULT output contains only '='. This was a dead contract — the schema listed values that never appeared in the output.

assay_type.description: ChEMBL assay type (B=Binding, F=Functional, U=Unclassified, A=ADME, P=Physicochemical, T=Toxicity).

drugbank_drugs.csv.description: DrugBank output: FDA-approved drug metadata. Produced by DrugBankPipeline.clean() (drugbank_pipeline.py). audit-2025 ROOT FIX (issue 6): the previous schema listed only 6 columns but the actual CSV has 35 columns. The missing 29 columns include patient-safety-critical fields (is_withdrawn, clinical_status, groups) and enrichment fields (mechanism_of_action, smiles, molecular_weight, max_phase, drug_type, chembl_id, pubchem_cid) that downstream Phase 2 consumers NEED. The schema was lying about the output contract.

inchikey.description: Canonical 27-char InChIKey. NULL for biologics without a calculable InChIKey.

is_withdrawn.description: PATIENT SAFETY: True if the drug has been withdrawn from the market. RL ranker must NOT recommend withdrawn drugs.

indication.description: Free-text indication (used for DrugBank-OMIM disease matching).

inchikey_source.description: How the InChIKey was obtained (rdkit, drugbank_xml, synthetic, etc.).

_smiles_used_for_recovery.description: Lineage: SMILES used for InChIKey recovery.

_inchikey_recovery_failed.description: Lineage: True if InChIKey recovery failed.

_inchikey_recovery_error.description: Lineage: error message from InChIKey recovery.

_is_fda_approved_was_filled.description: Lineage: True if is_fda_approved was filled from a fallback.

_mechanism_of_action_was_filled.description: Lineage: True if mechanism_of_action was filled from a fallback.

_molecular_formula_was_filled.description: Lineage: True if molecular_formula was filled from a fallback.

_smiles_was_filled.description: Lineage: True if smiles was filled from a fallback.

proteins.csv.description: UniProt output: reviewed human protein sequences. Produced by UniProtPipeline.clean() (uniprot_pipeline.py).

protein_protein_interactions.csv.description: STRING output: protein-protein interaction network. Produced by StringPipeline.clean() (string_pipeline.py).

gene_disease_associations.csv.description: DisGeNET output: curated gene-disease associations. Produced by DisGeNETPipeline.clean() (disgenet_pipeline.py). Schema extended (v2.0) to declare all institutional-grade columns as optional; existing required columns (gene_id, disease_id, score) are preserved. pmid_list is a semicolon-separated string of 7-8 digit integer PMIDs, sorted most-recent-first, deduped, capped at DISGENET_PMID_CAP. Score is the DisGeNET Disease-Specific Genomic Profile (DSGP) per Piñero et al. 2020, in [0, 1]; sub-weak evidence (score in [0.06, 0.1)) is preserved by default and tagged confidence_tier='weak'. Output is written with csv.QUOTE_ALL, UTF-8 encoding, and Unix line endings (\n).

omim_gene_disease_associations.csv.description: OMIM output: Mendelian gene-phenotype mappings (mapping_key in OMIM_MAPPING_KEYS_INCLUDE, default [3,4]). Produced by OMIMPipeline.clean() (omim_pipeline.py) — institutional-grade rewrite per OMIM_PIPELINE_MASTER_FIX_PROMPT.md.

evidence_strength.description: FORENSIC Chain 3 root fix: evidence_strength is a STRING label (one of 'robust'/'moderate'/'limited'/'unsupported'), NOT a number. The ORM (models.py:1322) declares it as String(20) with a CHECK constraint enforcing these four values, and DisGeNET's _compute_evidence_strength emits these labels. The previous schema declaration 'number' caused INSERT failures on PostgreSQL for any OMIM row with non-NULL evidence_strength (type/check error). On SQLite the mismatch was silently coerced, so tests passed but production failed.

pubchem_enrichment.csv.description: PubChem output: compound structural enrichment data. Produced by PubChemPipeline.clean() (pubchem_pipeline.py) — institutional-grade rewrite per PUBCHEM_PIPELINE_MASTER_FIX_PROMPT.md. All 15+ physicochemical properties fetched from PubChem PUG REST are persisted (previously 11 were silently dropped). Stereochemistry is preserved via separate canonical_smiles (no stereo) and isomeric_smiles (with stereo, isotopes, charge) columns. Predicted-vs-measured provenance is flagged via xlogp_source and tpsa_source. Empty strings from PubChem become SQL NULL (not ''). molecular_weight and exact_mass use Decimal precision. Output is written with csv.QUOTE_MINIMAL (P1-26 ROOT FIX — was QUOTE_NONNUMERIC; the asymmetry with the QUOTE_MINIMAL read path caused NaN to round-trip as empty strings), UTF-8 encoding, Unix line endings (\n), and SHA-256 + run-context sidecars by BasePipeline._persist_cleaned_data. Column order is fixed by COLUMN_ORDER in pubchem_pipeline.py.

inchikey.description: InChIKey requested from PubChem (verified to match response InChIKey before storage).

pubchem_cid.description: PubChem Compound ID (parent / standardized). Two different salt forms of the same drug share the same parent CID.

molecular_weight.description: Average MW using natural-abundance atomic weights (C=12.011, H=1.008). Decimal precision (NUMERIC(12,6)).

exact_mass.description: Monoisotopic mass (Da) — uses most-abundant isotope (C=12.000, H=1.0078). Use this for mass-spectrometry-based drug discovery.

canonical_smiles.description: PubChem CanonicalSMILES — no stereochemistry. NEVER coalesce with isomeric_smiles.

isomeric_smiles.description: PubChem IsomericSMILES — with stereochemistry, isotopes, formal charges. The Graph Transformer's molecular fingerprinting MUST use this. For chiral drugs the '@' token distinguishes enantiomers — life-safety-critical.

inchi.description: Full InChI string (structural + stereo + isotopes + charge).

iupac_name.description: PubChem IUPACName — may be PIN or non-PIN. See iupac_name_type for source attribution.

cas_number.description: CAS Registry Number from PubChem synonyms endpoint (when PUBCHEM_PIPELINE_FETCH_CAS=true). Cross-validated against drugs.cas_number (DrugBank).

xlogp.description: PubChem XLogP3 PREDICTION (not experimental). Group-contribution QSAR model — experimental logP can differ by 1+ log unit. See xlogp_source.

xlogp_source.description: Provenance flag — always 'pubchem_xlogp3' for fetched rows.

tpsa.description: PubChem-calculated Topological Polar Surface Area (not measured).

tpsa_source.description: Provenance flag — always 'pubchem_calculated' for fetched rows.

h_bond_donor_count.description: Lipinski-style H-bond donor count (N-H + O-H bonds). Approximate — for pharmacophore modeling recompute from SMILES with RDKit.

h_bond_acceptor_count.description: Lipinski-style H-bond acceptor count (N + O atoms). Approximate.

heavy_atom_count.description: PubChem heavy-atom count — EXCLUDES hydrogen (PubChem convention). For total atom count, compute from molecular_formula.

formal_charge.description: Formal charge parsed from isomeric_smiles (RDKit authoritative; SMILES token heuristic as fallback).

isotope_info.description: JSON dict of isotope labels parsed from isomeric_smiles, e.g. '{"F": 18, "C": 11}' for a PET tracer. NULL when no isotopes.

salt_form.description: V19 ROOT FIX (PS-1 / SW-2): derived from the full InChI string (multiple disconnected components combined with non-zero formal charge). Returns one of {neutral, protonated, deprotonated, zwitterion, salt_form} or None when the InChI is unavailable. The V18 N/M/P/S single-char mapping (which mis-labeled plain neutral molecules like aspirin as 'S=salt_form' because real InChIKeys almost always end in 'S'=Standard) is REMOVED — see pubchem_pipeline.py::_extract_salt_form.

protonation_state.description: V19 ROOT FIX (PS-1 / SW-2): protonation state derived from the full InChI string's /p and /q layers (NOT the InChIKey's last char, which is a 2-value version flag S/N — not a 4-state protonation flag). Returns one of {neutral, protonated, deprotonated, zwitterion, salt_form} or None when the InChI is unavailable. The V18 N/M/P/S enum is REMOVED — see pubchem_pipeline.py::_extract_protonation_state.

download_method.description: Fetch method: 'pug_rest_batch' for normal fetches, 'pug_rest_single' for split-retry.

pipeline_run_id.description: UUID4 of the pipeline run that produced this row.

input_checksum.description: SHA-256 of inchikeys_to_lookup.txt. Identical inputs produce identical checksums — supports reproducibility audits.

transformations.description: Semicolon-joined list of transformations applied (validated_inchikey_format; fetched_pubchem_properties; verified_response_inchikey_matches_request; sanitized_empty_strings_to_null; converted_molecular_weight_to_decimal; validated_ranges; deduplicated_by_inchikey_lowest_cid; extracted_protonation_state; extracted_isotope_info; computed_formal_charge).

as_of_date.description: Point-in-time requested by the caller (PubChem PUG REST does not support point-in-time queries — the value is recorded for traceability but ignored by the API).
